name: Sangonera la Seca to Puebla de Soto via Avenida del Príncipe
name_original: Sangonera la Seca a Puebla de Soto por la Avenida del Príncipe
region_intro: Jogging around Rio Segura offers diverse landscapes and natural features. The region is characterized by extensive riverside trails and green corridors along the Segura River, providing accessible paths for various fitness levels. Dramatic canyons, such as the Almadenes Canyon, showcase deep gorges and rich ecosystems, while some routes offer views of orchards and historical sites. The varied terrain includes both gentle gradients and paths with moderate elevation gains, catering to different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Rio Segura The most popular jogging route is Alcantarilla to Murcia via the Río Segura, an 11.4 miles (18.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the river, connecting urban areas with natural sections. Another top favourite among local runners is Puente de las Ovejas, La Contraparada y Ermita Nuestra Señora de la Salud — circular, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.8 km) path. This circular route offers views of historical sites and riverside scenery. Local runners also love the Río Segura Trail (GR 127), a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through riverside green corridors, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Jogging around Rio Segura is defined by riverside trails, green corridors, and dramatic canyons. The network offers options for both casual runners and more experienced athletes seeking varied terrain. The routes in Rio Segura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 8000 runners have used komoot to explore Rio Segura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Rio Segura region?
A: The Rio Segura region offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 500 trails available on komoot. These range from easy riverside paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging paths in Rio Segura?
A: Yes, the Rio Segura region has over 40 easy running routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. Many of these are along the extensive riverside trails and green corridors, offering gentle gradients and accessible paths. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1342492525/witches-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Witches' Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.1 km route with minimal elevation.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails around Rio Segura?
A: Runners in Rio Segura can enjoy diverse scenery, from picturesque riverside trails lined with elms, ashes, and willows, to dramatic landscapes like the Almadenes Canyon with its towering walls. Some routes also offer views of orchards, historical sites, and panoramic vistas from points like the Alto de Bayna or the Mirador de la ermita de la Virgen del Buen Suceso.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Rio Segura area?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. One popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1341927246/puente-de-las-ovejas-la-contraparada-y-ermita-nuestra-senora-de-la-salud-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de las Ovejas, La Contraparada y Ermita Nuestra Señora de la Salud — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.8 km path that offers views of historical sites and riverside scenery. Another challenging circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375846489/abaran-and-cerro-de-la-atalaya-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abarán and Cerro de la Atalaya Circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 22.2 km long.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Rio Segura?
A: The running trails in Rio Segura are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 8,000 runners have explored the region, often praising the diverse terrain, well-defined paths, and the beautiful natural and historical elements encountered along the way.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or attractions I can see while jogging in Rio Segura?
A: Absolutely! Many routes pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter historical bridges, or natural features like the Almadenes Canyon. Notable highlights in the wider region include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Relojero Peak&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Rio Segura?
A: The Rio Segura region offers pleasant running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially midday, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended during these months. Winters are mild, making it a good destination for year-round running.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow the GR 127 trail?
A: Yes, a significant draw for runners in Rio Segura is the presence of extensive riverside trails, including sections of the GR 127. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1355238890/rio-segura-trail-gr-127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Segura Trail (GR 127)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 9.8 km route that leads through these green corridors.

Q: Can I find running routes with good views in the Rio Segura region?
A: Many running routes in Rio Segura offer spectacular views. Locations such as the Alto de Bayna viewing point in Blanca provide panoramic views over the Azud reservoir and the Segura River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375215105/medina-siyasa-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medina Siyâsa Trail&lt;/a&gt; from Cieza also offers panoramic views from the Mirador de la ermita de la Virgen del Buen Suceso.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced athletes?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Rio Segura offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1341368060/alcantarilla-to-murcia-via-the-rio-segura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcantarilla to Murcia via the Río Segura&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.4 km trail. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374379759/sierra-de-ascoy-and-gr-7-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Ascoy and GR 7 Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 20 km with significant elevation.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs near the running trails?
A: Yes, the region features several lakes and reservoirs that can be incorporated into or viewed from running routes. Notable ones include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santomera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1553819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mayés Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes?
A: The terrain varies significantly, offering something for everyone. Many riverside paths are paved or accessible with gentle gradients, ideal for a smooth run. However, the region also features trails with moderate to significant elevation gains and some requiring good sure-footedness, particularly in areas like the Almadenes Canyon or mountainous loops.
tips: An Aviocar retired by the Air Force occupies the La Pradera roundabout, at the Alcantarilla exit in the direction of the air base.

The Segura River originates in Pontones (Jaén) at an altitude of 1,413 meters. Along its 325 kilometers of course, it crosses the provinces of Jaén, Albacete and Murcia before ending up on the Mediterranean coast, in the province of Alicante.
A small, cozy, and very well decorated hermitage. They usually open it between April and May.

waypoints.name: Aviocar sculpture at La Pradera roundabout
